prompt:
Perf ticket from the vibration-analysis pipeline. Package spanoverlap (this repo) finds which sensor capture windows were live at the same instant — Overlaps(spans, budget) returns every overlapping index pair, and cross-correlation consumes those pairs downstream. On bench rigs with a few hundred windows it's fine. A production rig dumps ~60k windows per shift, and the overlap pass now runs so long the shift report gets killed by its scheduler: the code literally weighs every window against every other window, ~1.8 billion pair examinations for inputs whose real overlap count is around sixty thousand.

Make it scale without changing any observable behavior. The contract, as pinned by the module docs and the tests: half-open [Start, End) windows, End <= Start is empty and overlaps nothing; result is every index pair (A < B) whose windows intersect, sorted ascending by A then B; and the Budget accounting stays honest — Checks increments once per candidate-pair examination (testing a specific pair OR emitting a pair you already know intersects), a nil *Budget means uncounted/unlimited, and when MaxChecks > 0 is exhausted you stop promptly with ErrBudget and a nil list. The perf suite runs the 60k-window shift under a MaxChecks budget of a few million: sixty-ish checks per window plus a handful per real overlap. Anything that examines every pair blows that budget three-hundred-fold; an approach that only ever weighs windows that can actually still intersect fits with room to spare and finishes the suite in seconds.

Keep it -race clean; go test -race -timeout 120s ./... is the gate and overlap_test.go must not change.
